---
title: "ASI Is a Stack, Not a Model"
chapter_id: "asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model"
part_id: "foundations-alignment-governance"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "viea"
  - "beastbrain"
  - "aletheia"
  - "talos"
  - "moecot"
  - "scf"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# ASI Is a Stack, Not a Model

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model` |
| Part | Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `viea`, `beastbrain`, `aletheia`, `talos`, `moecot`, `scf` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The book needs a single architecture frame for advanced AI systems that must plan, remember, verify, act, route work, compress representations, and improve under governance.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

A larger model, a prompt wrapper, or an agent loop does not by itself define authority boundaries, memory discipline, evidence ledgers, tool permissions, or safe replacement rules.

## Core claim

[asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.core, support: argument] Efficient ASI should be modeled as a governed stack of cooperating layers rather than as one undifferentiated model.

## Mechanism

- Define each layer by responsibility, interface, artifact, invariant, and failure mode.
- Treat the whole book as a reference architecture rather than a collection of standalone papers.
- Use source queues and evidence states to keep future writing runs context-loaded and honest.

## Interfaces

- Alignment and governance constrain every downstream layer.
- Planning, memory, reasoning, execution, routing, compression, and evidence exchange typed artifacts.
- Recursive improvement is a governed transition, not an ambient property of intelligence.

## Invariants

- Layer boundaries remain explicit.
- Claims carry support states.
- Reasoning ability never implies execution authority.

## Failure modes

- Anthology drift.
- Monolith drift.
- Evidence inflation before source notes or tests exist.

## Minimal implementation

A stack map, layer-boundary table, source crosswalk, and claim ledger that make the architecture navigable before prose is complete.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Layer-boundary audit | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Source-to-layer traceability review | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Claim-support label audit | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `beastbrain` | BeastBrain Cognitive Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Architecture lineage. Organism paradigm, SSD-native/geometrically verified intelligence. |
| `aletheia` | Aletheia Foundry | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Safe general intelligence predecessor. Use ideas; avoid final branding if collision remains. |
| `talos` | Talos Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: AI labor OS. Deterministic cognitive manufacturing, typed jobs, control planes, auditability, tool isolation. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |
| `scf` | Stable Capability Fields | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Use public release v1.0 when available. Stable boundaries, replacement, bounded authority, recoverable evolution. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
